crop.disambiguation_note: All sources verified to describe Coriandrum sativum (annual herb, oval-to-round leaves, white-pink umbel, round seed). Sources about ผักชีฝรั่ง (Eryngium foetidum), ผักชีลาว (Anethum graveolens), or ผักชีล้อม (Oenanthe javanica) were excluded.

sources.title: การปลูกผักชี พืชอายุสั้นทนแล้ง (Coriander cultivation: short-cycle drought-tolerant crop)
sources.authors: วสันต์ ชุณห์วิจิตรา, ยุพวัลย์ ทองใบอ่อน (Wasant Chunwijitra, Yupawun Thongbaiorn)
sources.verification_notes: WebFetch confirmed body identifies the work as multimedia content on Coriandrum sativum cultivation; not Eryngium / Anethum / Oenanthe.

sources.title: ผักชี (Coriander) — ภูมิปัญญาท้องถิ่น สำนักงานเกษตรและสหกรณ์จังหวัดพิจิตร
sources.publisher: Phichit Provincial Agriculture and Cooperatives Office (สำนักงานเกษตรและสหกรณ์ จังหวัดพิจิตร) — Ministry of Agriculture and Cooperatives
sources.verification_notes: WebFetch confirmed: Coriandrum sativa L. (Umbelliferae), 40-60 day annual; whole-plant utilization (leaf, seed, root); yield 1,300-1,900 kg/rai with organic manure; harvest 30-45 DAS.

sources.title: ผักชี (Parsley) — สำนักงานเกษตรและสหกรณ์จังหวัดพิจิตร (preview page)
sources.publisher: Phichit Provincial Agriculture and Cooperatives Office
sources.verification_notes: WebSearch + curl-200 confirmed; companion of the PDF entry above. Confirms 40-60 day cycle, soaking seed overnight to soften hard pericarp, row spacing 20-30 cm, dislikes intense direct sun.

sources.title: ทางเลือกปลูกกินเองที่บ้าน ยุคผักชีแพงมหาโหด — รออากาศหนาว
sources.publisher: Kanchanaburi Provincial Agriculture and Cooperatives Office (สำนักงานเกษตรและสหกรณ์จังหวัดกาญจนบุรี)
sources.verification_notes: WebFetch confirmed crop-specific: discusses growing cilantro at home during price-spike periods, waiting for cooler weather (รออากาศหนาว). Reinforces Thailand-specific cool-season strategy.

sources.title: ผักชี (coriander, cilantro) — ฐานข้อมูลงานวิจัยพืชผัก ศูนย์นวัตกรรมเทคโนโลยีหลังการเก็บเกี่ยว
sources.publisher: Postharvest Technology Innovation Center (ศูนย์นวัตกรรมเทคโนโลยีหลังการเก็บเกี่ยว), Chiang Mai University
sources.verification_notes: WebFetch confirmed: explicitly identifies ผักชี / Coriandrum sativum / Apiaceae and aggregates Thai academic postharvest research (1-MCP, ozone, temperature × storage life).

sources.title: 06. การปลูกและดูแลผักชี — ศูนย์วิทยบริการเพื่อส่งเสริมการเกษตร
sources.publisher: Department of Agricultural Extension (กรมส่งเสริมการเกษตร) — Agricultural Extension Service Center
sources.verification_notes: WebFetch confirmed page is specifically titled 'การปลูกและดูแลผักชี' (Coriandrum sativum, annual cilantro — explicitly distinguished from ผักชีฝรั่ง and ผักชีลาว). Infographic format means body detail extraction is limited; treat as authoritative summary, not deep technical reference.

sources.title: Coriandrum sativum L. (Apiaceae) — Thai Vegetables Database #036
sources.publisher: Japan International Research Center for Agricultural Sciences (JIRCAS), Tsukuba, Japan
sources.verification_notes: WebFetch confirmed: explicitly Thai-context vegetable database entry on Coriandrum sativum. States 'Prefers a cool climate, full sun, and sufficient soil moisture. Thrives in well-drained soil.' Foliage vulnerable to heavy precipitation.

sources.title: Cilantro / Coriander, Coriandrum sativum
sources.publisher: University of Wisconsin–Madison Division of Extension
sources.verification_notes: WebFetch confirmed: 'cool season crop... tends to bolt (go to seed) in hot weather'; recommends successive sowings every 2-3 weeks; summer plantings not recommended. Caveat: Wisconsin temperate context — adapt timing to Thai cool season (Nov–Feb).

sources.title: Coriander (Cilantro) — Diseases and Pests, Description, Uses, Propagation
sources.verification_notes: WebFetch confirmed: comprehensive crop-specific pest/disease list. States 'damp, cool springs and hot, dry summers at temperatures between 17 and 27°C', well-draining soil, sown 0.6-1.2 cm depth, harvest 45-70 DAS.

sources.title: Cilantro/Coriander in the Garden
sources.verification_notes: WebFetch confirmed: peer-reviewed extension fact sheet (Aug 2018). Recommends thinning to 2 in apart for leaf vs. 8-10 in for seed. Caveat: Utah temperate climate — Thailand should plant in cool season equivalent rather than spring/fall.

sources.title: Cilantro, a Unique Culinary Herb
sources.authors: Mandy L. Smith, Extension Educator, Westmoreland County
sources.verification_notes: WebFetch confirmed: explicitly names slow-bolt cultivars 'Calypso, Leisure, Confetti' and discusses afternoon shade strategy — directly addresses the Thai bolting-tolerance angle. Also covers OR6A2 soapy-taste genetic basis.

sources.title: Coriandrum sativum — Useful Tropical Plants
sources.publisher: Useful Tropical Plants Database (Ken Fern, Ajna Fern, 2014, CC BY-NC-SA 3.0)
sources.verification_notes: WebFetch confirmed: tropical-context entry. Critical note: 'rarely forms seeds at elevations lower than 500 metres' — directly relevant to Thai lowland seed production. Optimal 15-25°C, tolerates 4-32°C.

research_notes: Disambiguation strict: every source verified to describe Coriandrum sativum (annual cilantro), not ผักชีฝรั่ง (Eryngium foetidum) / ผักชีลาว (Anethum graveolens) / ผักชีล้อม (Oenanthe javanica). The OPSMOAC and ARDA blog system contains separate pages for ผักชีฝรั่ง and ผักชีลาว — those were explicitly excluded.

Bolting-tolerance angle is well-covered: Penn State Extension names slow-bolt cultivars (Calypso, Leisure, Confetti); Wisconsin and JIRCAS confirm cool-season preference; OPSMOAC Kanchanaburi explicitly addresses Thai cool-season planting strategy ('รออากาศหนาว'); Useful Tropical Plants notes tropical seed-set difficulty below 500m elevation.

Thai use of all three plant parts (leaf for garnish/soup-topping, root for ตำพริกแกง / curry pastes, seed เมล็ดผักชี for spice) is supported by OPSMOAC Phichit (whole-plant utilization) and Useful Tropical Plants.
'Split-the-seed' / soak-and-crack pre-treatment is a documented Thai practice supported by OPSMOAC Phichit and the Thai-language sources retrieved during search (corroborating practice).
